# Generated rubric — Knowledge Islands Streams zones

> **Generated publication.** The TypeScript rubric items under `scripts/rubric/items/` are canonical.

## STREAM — Streams structure

Focus layout, indexes, proposal suffixes, and placement.

- **STREAM-1 [M] — Focus folders** — Folders directly under Streams are canonical Focus folders. (references/Streams Structure Reference.md)
- **STREAM-2 [M] — Focus indexes** — Each present Focus carries a same-name index note. (references/Streams Structure Reference.md)
- **STREAM-3 [M] — proposal suffix** — Full proposals use the Proposal suffix while lightweight streams do not. (references/Streams Structure Reference.md)
- **STREAM-4 [J] — Focus index ordering** — Focus indexes carry correctly ordered Streams tables and one category convention.
  - _Review prompt:_ Are index tables current, ordered, and consistently categorised? (references/Streams Structure Reference.md)
- **STREAM-5 [J] — Focus placement** — Each stream sits under its real attention Focus.
  - _Review prompt:_ Do sampled streams match their actual attention Focus? (references/Streams Structure Reference.md)

## ENACT — Enactment Process

Proposal frontmatter, lifecycle, and settlement.

- **ENACT-1 [M] — proposal frontmatter** — Each proposal declares status, priority, and dependencies in closed frontmatter. (references/Streams Structure Reference.md)
- **ENACT-2 [M] — lifecycle status and priority** — Proposal status and priority are bare tokens from the controlled vocabularies. (references/Streams Structure Reference.md)
- **ENACT-3 [J] — Governance section** — Every stream note declares and links its bound process note.
  - _Review prompt:_ Do sampled stream notes carry an appropriate Governance section? (references/Streams Structure Reference.md)
- **ENACT-4 [J] — index accuracy** — Focus and proposal indexes match the live streams and statuses.
  - _Review prompt:_ Do indexes accurately reflect live streams and statuses? (references/Streams Structure Reference.md)
- **ENACT-5 [J] — completed-proposal settlement** — Completed proposals are deleted after their output reaches a canonical zone.
  - _Review prompt:_ Have completed proposals been deleted only after their output settled into the appropriate store? (references/Streams Structure Reference.md)

## GATE — always-loaded gate

The canonical-change gate anchor.

- **GATE-1 [M] — always-loaded Enactment gate** — A base with proposals anchors the Enactment Process and proposal gate in root CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md. (references/Streams Structure Reference.md)
- **GATE-2 [J] — imperative gate directive** — The anchor is imperative and states the gate exemptions.
  - _Review prompt:_ Is the anchor a genuine imperative directive with the appropriate exemptions? (references/Streams Structure Reference.md)

## CONFIG — Streams configuration

The skill-owned ki-kb-streams table.

- **CONFIG-1 [M] — known Streams configuration** — Only process_note and note_type_scheme are recognised under ki-kb-streams. (references/Streams Structure Reference.md)
- **CONFIG-2 [M] — note type scheme** — note_type_scheme is type or tags when declared. (references/Streams Structure Reference.md)
